headline: Historian Pascal Blanchard: 'Identity will be one of the main themes of France's presidential campaign'
subhead: The issue of identity, tackled head-on by both the far-right and the radical left, calls for political solutions to heal divisions as the 2027 presidential election approaches, explains Pascal Blanchard, an expert in colonization, in an interview.

Uber Eats and Deliveroo accused of 'human trafficking': Multinational giants out of touch with delivery workers' struggles
subhead: A legal complaint against the two meal delivery platforms highlights the vast gap between the strategies of these publicly traded companies and the working conditions of their couriers, whose welfare they refuse to take responsibility for.

headline: Bernard Arnault defuses speculation over his succession at LVMH shareholders meeting
subhead: At 77 years old, the CEO of the luxury group refuses to disclose who will replace him.

headline: When screens disrupt interactions between parents and babies
subhead: 'Technoference' is now being studied by French researchers, who have observed a 'decline in parental sensitivity' and highlight that 'babies learn by observing but also by being observed.'

headline: 'How can anyone love Céline Dion? The hysteria surrounding her Paris concerts is astonishing'
subhead: After the spectacular sale of nearly 500,000 tickets for a series of shows in Paris this autumn, Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin questions in his column what drives people to love (or hate) the singer.

headline: Léon Marchand prepares return to French pools: 'I always told myself the Paris Games were just one step'
subhead: The 23-year-old swimmer, who won four Olympic golds in 2024, is set to compete at the French Championships in Saint-Etienne in July, followed by the European Championships in August in Saint-Denis.

headline: Sarkozy and the mystery of the Libyan money: A look back at a legal marathon
subhead: The appeals trial over suspicions of Libyan funding for the 2007 presidential campaign began on March 16. The case is filled with a cast of shady figures.
